question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words:

Liberal, Equal, Education, Dream, Noble
solution_html:
Liberal: She holds liberal views on social issues.

Equal: All men are created equal.

Education: Education is the backbone of a nation.

Dream: All men dream, but not equally.

Noble: She comes of/from a noble family.

Mark distribution:

For making meaningful correct sentences; (0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks.

question_html:
Change the following words into verb:

Proof, Sale, Speech, Reduction, Presumption
solution_html:
Proof – Prove ;

Sale – Sell ;

Speech – Speak ;

Reduction – Reduce ;

Presumption – Presume;

Mark Distribution:

For changing the following words into verbs (0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks.

question_html: Write a paragraph on “Education and Values”.
solution_html:
Education and Values

Education plays a vital role in shaping a person's character and future. It goes beyond books and exams to teach values that guide behavior. Honesty, respect, and responsibility are essential values learned through good education. These values help build trust and strong relationships in society. Education also teaches empathy and tolerance, which promote peace and understanding. Schools and families must work together to instill these principles. Without values, knowledge alone can be misused. True education develops both the mind and the heart. It creates responsible citizens who contribute positively to the world. Values make education meaningful and life-worthy.

Mark Distribution:

For writing the Title "Education and Values" – 0.5 mark.

For mentioning key aspects/sources related to education and values (e.g., honesty, respect, responsibility, empathy, tolerance, role of schools and families, etc.) – 01 mark.

For describing the importance of education and values

(e.g., shaping character, guiding behavior, promoting peace, building trust, creating responsible citizens, etc.) – 3.5 marks.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

* For every 3 mistakes (grammar/spelling), 01 mark has to be deducted.]
